subject: Accounting
title: Bad Debts and Write-Offs
description: Learn Bad Debts and Write-Offs in one minute.

hook.text: A customer declares bankruptcy. The $10,000 they owe you is never coming. Now what?

buildup.text: Bad debt expense recognizes that some receivables will never be collected.

discovery.text: Companies estimate bad debts using historical rates or aging analysis and create an allowance account.

twist.text: Writing off a receivable doesn't mean giving up—you can still pursue legal action to collect.

climax.text: Proper bad debt accounting keeps receivable balances honest and prevents overstated assets.

punchline.text: Not every customer pays. Plan for it.

quiz.question: Why do companies estimate bad debts?
